{"id":"bdcompound-z2f","title":"Enforce memory recall and knowledge capture as non-skippable gates","description":"Agent admitted to deliberately skipping beads-parallel section 8 (recall before spawning subagents) and beads-work Phase 1 step 2 (recall before implementation). The instructions are clear but treated as optional. Need to make these structurally harder to skip:

1. beads-parallel section 8: Make recall a required output checkpoint. Agent must output recall results (or 'No relevant knowledge found') before template placeholders can be filled. Add explicit note that `{recall_results}` in the agent prompt template is a required fill — leaving it empty/unfilled is a protocol violation.

2. beads-work Phase 1 step 2: Add required output gate. Agent must output recall results before proceeding to Phase 1 step 3. Change language from suggestion to gate.

3. Both agent prompt templates in beads-parallel: Add recall reminder at top of subagent prompts so even if orchestrator skips section 8, the subagent itself sees it as a first step.

4. beads-work knowledge capture: The subagent-wrapup.sh hook enforces this for subagents. But when beads-work runs as the main agent (not a subagent), no enforcement exists. Consider what can be done at the prompt level since there is no SessionEnd blocking hook.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T07:03:04Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-02-23T07:03:04Z"}

new_value: Strengthened recall as required gate in beads-work Phase 1 step 2 and beads-parallel section 8. Added fallback recall step 1 inside all subagent prompt templates (default and ralph). Fixed ralph template step numbering.

{"id":"bdcompound-bb9","title":"Fix: beads-plan-review apply feedback is incomplete and unverified","description":"When user selects 'Apply feedback' after a plan review, the agent has no instructions for how to enumerate, apply, and verify all recommendations. The result is that some recommendations get silently skipped, and the user believes everything was applied when it wasn't.

Fix: Replace the vague 'Apply feedback' option with a rigorous application protocol that:
1. Builds an explicit numbered checklist of ALL recommendations from the review
2. Works through each item systematically, updating the relevant child bead
3. Does a completeness check before reporting done - comparing applied changes against the original list

Also needs to cover beads-deepen which has a similar gap: updates child beads but no verification that all agent recommendations were actually incorporated.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:38:38Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:38:38Z"}

new_value: Added Step A-D apply feedback protocol to beads-plan-review.md and completeness verification to beads-deepen.md

{"description":"## Problem

The current workflow for thorough feature planning requires running 4 commands manually in
sequence:

1. /beads-brainstorm -- collaborative dialogue exploring WHAT to build, producing a brainstorm
bead with DECISION/INVESTIGATION/FACT/PATTERN comments
2. /beads-plan -- transforms the idea into a structured epic with child task beads, each with
detailed descriptions (What/Context/Testing/Validation/Files/Dependencies)
3. /beads-deepen -- enriches every child bead with parallel multi-agent research (20-40+ agents),
applying skill discoveries, learnings, and review/research/design agent outputs
4. /beads-plan-review -- dispatches 4 review agents (architecture-strategist, code-simplicity-
reviewer, security-sentinel, performance-oracle) to catch issues before implementation

Each command produces artifacts (bead IDs, knowledge comments, enriched descriptions) that the
next command consumes. The user must manually pass context between them and remember the correct
sequence. This friction discourages using the full pipeline.

## Solution

A new /beads-design command that orchestrates the entire planning pipeline as a single
invocation, without skipping steps or losing functionality.

## Architecture Decisions

### Delegation, not duplication

/beads-design is a pure orchestrator that calls the 4 existing commands in sequence. Zero code
duplication. When individual commands are updated or improved, /beads-design automatically
inherits those improvements. The command file itself contains only orchestration logic: phase
transitions, progress banners, verification gates, and the final summary.

### Smart pauses (user interaction model)

• Phase 1 (Brainstorm): Fully interactive -- the brainstorm phase requires collaborative
dialogue (questions, approach selection). Cannot be automated.
• Phase 2 (Plan): Runs automatically after brainstorm completes, consuming brainstorm output. No
user interaction needed.
• Transition pause: After plan completes, the orchestrator shows a plan summary and asks the
user to confirm before investing heavy compute in deepen. This is the single checkpoint between
the interactive and autonomous portions.
• Phase 3+4 (Deepen + Plan-Review): Auto-chains without stopping. Deepen output feeds directly
into plan-review.

### Phase gates with recovery

Each phase runs its existing verification logic. If verification fails at any phase, the
orchestrator pauses and presents three options: retry the phase, skip it and continue, or abort
the pipeline. This prevents wasted compute on downstream phases when an upstream phase has
issues.

### Auto-apply safe feedback

After plan-review runs, non-controversial feedback (typos, missing test cases, documentation
gaps,
straightforward improvements) is applied automatically to child beads. The orchestrator only
pauses for trade-off decisions that require user judgment (e.g., architectural alternatives,
scope changes, performance vs. simplicity trade-offs).

### Default Comprehensive detail level

Since /beads-design is the full-thoroughness pipeline, the plan phase defaults to Comprehensive
detail level. The user can override this inline (e.g., /beads-design standard "feature idea")
but the default matches the intent of using the full pipeline.

### No skip flags, auto-detect context

If the user passes a bead ID that already has brainstorm context (brainstorm label, DECISION
comments), the brainstorm phase detects this and skips itself. No --skip-brainstorm or --
from=plan flags needed. The interface stays simple: /beads-design "feature idea" or /beads-design {bead-
id}.

### Parallelism preserved

No new parallelism constraints introduced. Each delegated command retains its internal
parallelism:

• /beads-plan spawns parallel research agents (repo-research-analyst + learnings-researcher,
optionally best-practices-researcher + framework-docs-researcher)
• /beads-deepen spawns 20-40+ agents across skill discovery, learnings application, per-section
research, and all review/research/design agents
• /beads-plan-review runs 4 review agents concurrently
Model hints (haiku for lightweight research, sonnet for analysis, opus for deep review) are
inherited from existing commands.

### Output style

• Progress banners at each phase transition (e.g., '=== Phase 2/4: Planning... ===')
• Clear verification status per phase (pass/fail with details)
• Final consolidated summary: epic bead ID, child bead count, key decisions captured, review
findings applied, and suggested next step (/beads-work or /beads-parallel)

## Precedent

Follows the /lfg command pattern, which already chains plan -> deepen -> work -> review ->
resolve-todo-parallel -> test-browser -> feature-video as a compound command.

## Usage

/beads-design "feature idea or description" /beads-design {existing-bead-id}

## Success Criteria

• Running /beads-design produces identical artifacts to running the 4 commands manually
• No functionality is lost from any individual command
• User interaction is reduced to: brainstorm dialogue + one plan confirmation + trade-off
decisions only
• Total pipeline context (bead IDs, knowledge comments, enriched descriptions) flows correctly
between all phases
• Phase gate recovery works correctly (retry/skip/abort)"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.9","title":"Paths starting with '-' silently treated as flags in install.js TARGET_PATH parsing","description":"## Issue
In bin/install.js:52-54, positional path detection uses `args.find(a => !a.startsWith('-'))`. A path like `-my-project` is silently dropped, falling through to wrong target. Also, `args.find()` is called twice redundantly.

## Severity
P2 — Silent misdirection to wrong install target

## Location
bin/install.js:52-54

## Fix
```js
const positional = args.find((a) => !a.startsWith("-"));
const TARGET_PATH = positional ? path.resolve(positional) : null;
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] args.find() called only once
- [ ] Paths starting with '-' produce a clear error rather than silent drop","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:43:38Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:43:38Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.15","title":"plan-export.js --help exits with code 1 instead of 0","description":"## Issue
bin/plan-export.js usage() calls process.exit(1) for --help, inconsistent with plan-view.js which exits 0. Breaks `cmd --help && do_something` shell idioms.

## Location
bin/plan-export.js:27

## Fix
Change `process.exit(1)` to `process.exit(0)` in the usage() function.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] `bd-plan-export --help` exits with code 0
- [ ] Consistent with plan-view.js","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:18Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:18Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.17","title":"plan-view.js contentHeight can go negative on tiny terminals","description":"## Issue
bin/plan-view.js: `contentHeight = rows - 3` goes negative on 2-row terminals. No crash but renders only header/footer with no content visible.

## Location
bin/plan-view.js: terminal height calculation

## Fix
`const contentHeight = Math.max(0, rows - 3);`

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No negative contentHeight on any terminal size
- [ ] No crash on tiny/minimal terminals","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:28Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:28Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.10","title":"GITHUB_WORKSPACE unquoted in CI — breaks on self-hosted runners with spaces in path","description":"## Issue
In .github/workflows/test-installation.yml, `node $GITHUB_WORKSPACE/bin/install.js` is unquoted. If a self-hosted runner has spaces in the workspace path, this splits into multiple shell tokens and fails.

## Severity
P2 — Latent failure on self-hosted runners

## Location
.github/workflows/test-installation.yml (npx installer job, node invocation line)

## Fix
```yaml
node "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/bin/install.js" --claude --yes
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] All $GITHUB_WORKSPACE references in shell steps are quoted","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:43:44Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:43:44Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.12","title":"npm publish missing --provenance and --access public flags","description":"## Issue
.github/workflows/npm-publish.yml publishes without:
1. `--provenance` flag (SLSA attestation linking package to CI run/commit)
2. `--access public` flag (explicit scope for scoped packages)

## Severity
P2 — Supply chain hygiene gap; no cryptographic link from published package to source

## Location
.github/workflows/npm-publish.yml: npm publish step

## Fix
Add to job permissions: `id-token: write`
Change publish command: `npm publish --provenance --access public`

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Published package shows provenance attestation on npmjs.com
- [ ] --access public prevents accidental private publish for scoped packages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:43:59Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:43:59Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.16","title":"args.find() called twice in install.js TARGET_PATH parsing (code quality)","description":"## Issue
bin/install.js:52-54 calls args.find() twice with identical predicate — redundant evaluation.

## Location
bin/install.js:52-54

## Fix
```js
const positional = args.find((a) => !a.startsWith("-"));
const TARGET_PATH = positional ? path.resolve(positional) : null;
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] args.find() called once
- [ ] TARGET_PATH behavior unchanged","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:23Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:23Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.13","title":"execSync timeout missing in plan-export.js — hangs indefinitely on stuck bd","description":"## Issue
bin/plan-export.js bdExecFile calls have no timeout. Unlike plan-view.js (which sets timeout: 10000), a stuck bd process will block forever.

## Location
bin/plan-export.js: bdExecFile function

## Fix
Add timeout to execFileSync options: `{ encoding: 'utf8', stdio: [...], timeout: 30000 }`

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] plan-export.js exits with error if bd takes >30s","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:04Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:04Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.18","title":"plan-view.js detail panel truncates long bead descriptions (magic +20 buffer)","description":"## Issue
bin/plan-view.js renderDetailPanel uses `contentHeight + 20` as a magic number for scroll headroom. Beads with 50+ line descriptions get silently truncated.

## Location
bin/plan-view.js: renderDetailPanel function

## Fix
Remove the hard height cap from renderDetailPanel, or use a larger scroll buffer that adapts to content length.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Long bead descriptions (50+ lines) fully visible via scrolling
- [ ] No arbitrary truncation","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:33Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:33Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.14","title":"Bun version unpinned in CI (bun-version: latest)","description":"## Issue
.github/workflows/test-installation.yml uses `bun-version: latest` and `oven-sh/setup-bun@v1` (mutable tags). Unpinned versions can introduce unexpected breakage.

## Location
.github/workflows/test-installation.yml: Setup Bun step

## Fix
Pin to specific version: `bun-version: '1.2.5'` and pin action to commit SHA.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Bun version pinned to specific semver
- [ ] Action pinned to commit SHA or specific tag","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:12Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:12Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.19","title":"plan-view.js redundant process.on('exit', cleanup) registration","description":"## Issue
bin/plan-view.js registers `process.on('exit', cleanup)` but all exit paths already call cleanup() explicitly. The handler fires redundantly on every exit.

## Location
bin/plan-view.js: process.on('exit') registration

## Fix
Remove the `process.on('exit', cleanup)` registration. Keep the explicit cleanup() calls before each process.exit().

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] cleanup() called exactly once per exit
- [ ] Terminal state correctly restored in all exit scenarios","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:39Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:44:39Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.20","title":"plan-view.js: die() doesn't call cleanup() — terminal left in broken state","description":"## Issue
The die() function in bin/plan-view.js calls process.exit(1) without first calling cleanup(). When buildTree fails (e.g., bead not found), the terminal is left in raw mode with the cursor hidden, requiring the user to run reset or stty sane to recover.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Corrupts the user's terminal on bead lookup failure.

## Location
- bin/plan-view.js:451 — die() calls process.exit(1) with no cleanup
- bin/plan-view.js:134 — die() called from buildTree after raw mode is already set (line 487)

## Root Cause
process.on('exit', cleanup) was removed in commit 873d9f1. Previously it was the catch-all. Now cleanup() is only called from keyboard handlers. The die() path bypasses all keyboard handlers.

## Why This Matters
Any user running bd-plan-view BD-NONEXISTENT will get a corrupted terminal. Raw mode stays on, cursor stays hidden. This is the most common error path.

## Fix Options
1. Restore process.on('exit', cleanup) (one line, no side effects)
2. Call cleanup() inside die() before process.exit(1)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Running bd-plan-view BD-NONEXISTENT does not leave terminal in broken state
- [ ] Cursor is visible after exit
- [ ] Terminal input echoing works normally after failed lookup

## Testing Steps
1. Run: node bin/plan-view.js BD-NONEXISTENT
2. Expected: error message, terminal fully restored
3. NOT expected: cursor missing, input echoing broken","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:05:51Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:05:51Z"}

new_value: Restored process.on('exit', cleanup). Investigation showed die() calls precede setRawMode in current code, but the handler is valuable defensive practice for future changes.

{"id":"bdcompound-807.21","title":"CI: continue-on-error on installation test step swallows real failures","description":"## Issue
In .github/workflows/test-installation.yml, the 'Run installation tests' step has continue-on-error: true. This means even when beads is available and tests actually run, a genuine test failure does not fail CI. The step also explicitly does exit 0 in its catch block, creating a double guard that guarantees CI always stays green regardless of test outcome.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Real installation regressions pass CI silently.

## Location
- .github/workflows/test-installation.yml: 'Run installation tests' step (~line 57-70)

## Why This Matters
Making a step conditional (if: steps.check-beads.outputs.available == 'true') correctly handles the optional external dependency. But adding continue-on-error: true on top means: when beads IS available and tests run, failures are still ignored. This defeats the purpose of the test step.

## Fix
Remove continue-on-error: true from the 'Run installation tests' step. The conditional guard already handles beads being unavailable. If beads is installed and the test fails, that should be a real CI failure.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Removing continue-on-error and running CI confirms green on a correct installation
- [ ] A deliberate test failure (e.g., break a directory path assertion) causes CI to go red

## Testing Steps
1. Remove continue-on-error: true from the step
2. Push and verify green run
3. Introduce deliberate failure, verify CI goes red","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:03Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:03Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.24","title":"pre-release-check.sh OpenCode/Gemini thresholds stuck at 25 (CI uses 29) — SYNC violation","description":"## Issue
scripts/pre-release-check.sh checks OpenCode and Gemini command counts at >= 25, but .github/workflows/test-installation.yml (verify-release job) checks >= 29. Both files have a SYNC: comment requiring them to stay in sync. They are out of sync.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — The local pre-release script gives a false pass (25 is achievable with 4 missing commands), while CI will fail. This makes the pre-release script useless as a local gate.

## Location
- scripts/pre-release-check.sh: lines 105-106 (threshold 25 for OpenCode and Gemini)
- .github/workflows/test-installation.yml: lines 201, 204 (threshold 29)

## Fix
Update scripts/pre-release-check.sh OpenCode and Gemini thresholds from 25 to 29 to match CI.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] pre-release-check.sh and test-installation.yml use the same threshold (29) for all three output formats
- [ ] Running pre-release-check.sh locally gives the same pass/fail result as CI would

## Testing Steps
1. Update lines 105-106 in pre-release-check.sh to use 29
2. Run: bash scripts/pre-release-check.sh
3. Verify it passes (same as CI would)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:36Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:36Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.25","title":"plan-view.js: bdExecFile timeout 10s causes silent tree truncation on slow bd calls","description":"## Issue
bdExecFile in bin/plan-view.js uses timeout: 10000 (10 seconds) while the equivalent function in bin/plan-export.js uses timeout: 30000 (30 seconds). On a slow system or large bead tree, a 10s timeout causes bdExecFile to silently return empty string, which plan-view.js interprets as a missing bead — silently dropping phases or tasks from the displayed tree.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Silent data loss: users see an incomplete tree with no error indication.

## Location
- bin/plan-view.js:65 — timeout: 10000
- bin/plan-export.js:82 — timeout: 30000

## Why This Matters
The failure mode in plan-view.js is worse than in plan-export.js. In plan-export.js a timeout aborts with an error message. In plan-view.js the error is silently swallowed (bdExecFile returns empty string), so the TUI shows a truncated tree as if the missing beads don't exist. Users have no way to know data is missing.

## Fix
Raise timeout in plan-view.js bdExecFile to 30000ms to match plan-export.js.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Both plan-view.js and plan-export.js use timeout: 30000
- [ ] On a slow system, plan-view waits longer rather than silently truncating

## Testing Steps
1. Update timeout in plan-view.js bdExecFile to 30000
2. Verify plan-view still works normally on fast systems","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:47Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:47Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.26","title":"Stale command counts in CLAUDE.md, plugin-catalog.md, and marketplace.json","description":"## Issue
Three internal documentation files still show stale command counts after commands were bumped to 29:

1. CLAUDE.md:
   - Line 11: '28 commands for brainstorming...' → should be 29
   - Line 39: '# 28 commands' in directory tree → should be 29

2. .claude/rules/plugin-catalog.md:
   - Line 8: '## Commands (26)' → should be 29
   - Table body missing beads-design, beads-quick, beads-import entries

3. .claude-plugin/marketplace.json:
   - description field: '25 workflow commands' → should be 29 (matching plugin.json wording)

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Confusing for contributors reading these files, but doesn't affect users.

## Location
- CLAUDE.md: lines 11, 39
- .claude/rules/plugin-catalog.md: line 8 + table body
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json: description field

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] All three files show 29 commands
- [ ] plugin-catalog.md table includes beads-design, beads-quick, beads-import
- [ ] marketplace.json wording matches plugin.json

## Testing Steps
1. Update all three files
2. Verify: grep -r '25 workflow\|28 commands\|26\)' CLAUDE.md .claude/ .claude-plugin/","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:57Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:57Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.22","title":"npm-publish.yml: no needs:verify-release — can publish while CI verification failing","description":"## Issue
The npm-publish workflow has no needs: verify-release dependency. This means npm publish can run and succeed while the verify-release job (which checks version consistency, component counts, and conversion outputs) is still running or has failed.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Can publish a broken or incorrectly-versioned package to npm.

## Location
- .github/workflows/npm-publish.yml: publish job has no needs: clause referencing verify-release

## Why This Matters
The verify-release job exists precisely to gate releases. Running npm-publish without waiting for it means the gate can be bypassed on any release. A version mismatch or missing conversion outputs would result in a published package that fails to install correctly.

## Fix
Add needs: [verify-release] to the publish job in npm-publish.yml. This requires that verify-release runs first and passes before npm publish executes.

Note: verify-release is in test-installation.yml which is a separate workflow. Cross-workflow dependencies may require using workflow_run trigger or a combined workflow. Evaluate the simplest approach.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] npm-publish does not run if verify-release fails
- [ ] CI order is deterministic: verify first, publish second

## Testing Steps
1. Add needs dependency
2. Create a test release tag
3. Verify verify-release runs before publish","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:15Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:15Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.23","title":"CI: curl|bash to install beads has no SHA pin — supply chain risk","description":"## Issue
Both npx installer test jobs in test-installation.yml install the beads CLI via:
  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

This pins to the HEAD of the main branch, not to a specific commit SHA. If the steveyegge/beads repo is compromised, renamed, or its install script is maliciously modified, arbitrary code runs in the CI runner with access to NPM_TOKEN and other repo secrets.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Supply chain risk in CI environment.

## Location
- .github/workflows/test-installation.yml: Install beads CLI steps (~lines 55, 110)

## Why This Matters
curl|bash from a mutable URL is the canonical supply chain attack vector. The CI runner has NPM_TOKEN in scope.

## Fix Options
1. Pin to a specific commit SHA: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/<SHA>/scripts/install.sh
2. Vendor the install script into this repo and use a local path
3. Add a checksum verification step after download before execution

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] The install script URL is pinned to a specific SHA or checksum-verified
- [ ] A compromised HEAD of steveyegge/beads main does not affect this CI

## Testing Steps
1. Pin the URL to a known-good SHA
2. Verify CI still passes with the pinned URL","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:26Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:06:26Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.27","title":"Code cleanup: validateBeadId called per-function instead of once at entry","description":"## Issue
validateBeadId() is called at every individual fetch function (fetchBead, fetchChildren, fetchComments in plan-export.js; bdShowJson, bdListChildren, bdCommentsList in plan-view.js) instead of once at the entry point where the bead ID enters the program. This is redundant since execFileSync already prevents injection regardless, and the ID doesn't change between calls.

Additional code cleanup items in the same files:
1. plan-export.js: require statements appear after validateBeadId function definition (lines 15-16 should be at top)
2. plan-view.js: validateBeadId uses process.stderr.write instead of console.error (inconsistent with rest of file)
3. plan-view.js: usage() has no exitCode param unlike plan-export.js — callers must follow up with die() creating double-error output
4. install.js: _positional naming has underscore prefix suggesting 'unused' but is used immediately
5. plan-view.js: SIGINT handler is vestigial in raw mode (Ctrl-C comes through as keypress, not signal)

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Code clarity and consistency.

## Location
- bin/plan-export.js:92, 103, 113 (redundant validateBeadId calls)
- bin/plan-view.js:75, 86, 98 (redundant validateBeadId calls)
- bin/plan-export.js:15-16 (require placement)
- bin/plan-view.js:11 (stderr.write vs console.error)
- bin/plan-view.js:455-469 (usage() no exitCode)
- bin/install.js:52 (_positional naming)
- bin/plan-view.js:501-508 (vestigial SIGINT handler)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] validateBeadId called once at top of main execution path
- [ ] require statements grouped at top of plan-export.js
- [ ] Consistent console.error usage in validateBeadId
- [ ] usage() consistent between plan-view.js and plan-export.js

## Testing Steps
1. After changes, test both bin/plan-view.js and bin/plan-export.js with valid and invalid bead IDs
2. Verify --help shows usage and exits 0
3. Verify invalid bead ID exits 1 with clear error","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:07:15Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:07:15Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-807.28","title":"CI: Cortex conversion not verified in verify-release job","description":"## Issue
scripts/pre-release-check.sh checks Cortex conversion output (>= 25 .md files), but the verify-release job in .github/workflows/test-installation.yml has no equivalent Cortex check. The test-compatibility job generates Cortex outputs but doesn't validate them. Cortex conversion failures would not block a release.

## Secondary issues in the same area:
- bun install in CI verify-release job (line 156) doesn't use --frozen-lockfile, inconsistent with pre-release-check.sh which does use --frozen-lockfile
- verify-release only triggers on release events, not on push to main — component count regressions only caught at release time

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Cortex is a supported platform; its conversion should be gated.

## Location
- .github/workflows/test-installation.yml: verify-release job (missing Cortex check, missing --frozen-lockfile)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] verify-release checks Cortex .md command count >= 29
- [ ] bun install in verify-release uses --frozen-lockfile
- [ ] Consider adding verify-release trigger on push to main

## Testing Steps
1. Add Cortex check to verify-release job
2. Add --frozen-lockfile to bun install step
3. Verify CI still passes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:07:26Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:07:26Z"}
